old_exact:
established:

II

judgment

"Summary

is a procedural device under
N.D.R.Civ.P. 56(c) for promptly resolving a controversy on the
merits without a trial if there are no genuine issues of material
fact or inferences that can reasonably be drawn from undisputed
facts, or if the only issues to be resolved are questions of law. The
party seeking summary judgment must demonstrate there are no
genuine issues of material fact and the case is appropriate for
judgment as a matter of law. In deciding whether the district court
appropriately granted summary judgment, we view the evidence
in the light most favorable to the opposing party, giving that party
the benefit of all favorable inferences which can reasonably be
drawn from the record. A party opposing a motion for summary
judgment cannot simply rely on the pleadings or on unsupported
conclasory allegations. Rather, a party opposing a summary
judgment motion must present competent admissible evidence by
affidavit or other comparable means that raises an issue of
material fact and must, if appropriate, draw the court's attention
to relevant evidence in the record raising an issue of material fact.
When reasonable persons can reach only one conclusion from the
evidence, a question of fact may become a matter of law for the
court to decide. A district court's decision on summary judgment
is a question of law that we review de novo on the record."

source_quote:
well established:

             "Summary judgment is a procedural device under
      N.D.R.Civ.P. 56(c) for promptly resolving a controversy on the
      merits without a trial if there are no genuine issues of material
      fact or inferences that can reasonably be drawn from undisputed
      facts, or if the only issues to be resolved are questions of law. The
      party seeking summary judgment must demonstrate there are no
      genuine issues of material fact and the case is appropriate for
      judgment as a matter of law. In deciding whether the district court
      appropriately granted summary judgment, we view the evidence
      in the light most favorable to the opposing party, giving that party
      the benefit of all favorable inferences which can reasonably be
      drawn from the record. A party opposing a motion for summary
      judgment cannot simply rely on the pleadings or on unsupported
      conclusory allegations. Rather, a party opposing a summary
      judgment motion must present competent admissible evidence by
      affidavit or other comparable means that raises an issue of
      material fact and must, if appropriate, draw the court's attention
      to relevant evidence in the record raising an issue of material fact.
      When reasonable persons can reach only one conclusion from the
      evidence, a question of fact may become a matter of law for the
      court to decide. A district court's decision on summary judgment
      is a question of law that we review de novo on the record."
note: The word 'judgment' appears orphaned on its own line in the DB text between the section marker 'II' and the continuation 'Summary'. In the PDF source, these flow properly as 'Summary judgment'. This is a text reconstruction/split_join defect where the long quote block was improperly fragmented during OCR/ingestion. The proposed fix removes the stray 'judgment' line and the intervening section marker, restoring the quote to its proper form. Note: the long block necessarily includes the entire quote because the boundary (establishing 'judgment' as the singleton error) occurs within the quote context.

old_exact: Prosser states the 'It follows that the statute of proposition succinctly, limitations
new_exact: Prosser states the proposition succinctly, 'It follows that the statute of limitations
source_quote: establish a cause of action for malpractice. Prosser states the proposition succinctly, 'It follows that the statute of limitations does not begin to run against a negligence
evidence: The DB text has a split-join error in paragraph 11 where 'It follows' appears immediately after 'the' and 'proposition succinctly,' is inserted mid-clause. The PDF source, when normalized for layout, clearly shows the correct word order: 'Prosser states the proposition succinctly, 'It follows that the statute of limitations'. This is a transcription defect where text was joined in the wrong sequence during OCR/ingestion processing. The error is confirmed by: (1) exact byte-for-byte match of old_exact string in DB (occurs exactly once), (2) verification that the new_exact string appears in the PDF source when cleaned of layout formatting, (3) confirmation that this is part of a long block quote from Wall v. Lewis and the sentence requires 'proposition succinctly,' to come after 'states the' for grammatical coherence.
note: High-confidence fix: split_join error (wrongly fused/re-ordered text). The phrase structure 'Prosser states the proposition succinctly' is a complete thought that introduces the quote that follows. The old version breaks this by placing 'It follows' too early and 'proposition succinctly' in the middle of a clause, making the sentence nonsensical. PDF source confirms the correct sequencing.

source_quote: He stated, "[N]ow my grandpa said he never put that $100 bill in there, but I knew he was lying."
source: Legal writing convention and Bluebook citation format
evidence: The bracketed letter in legal quotations should use capital form when indicating a capitalization correction. Lowercase [n] is not standard legal writing form; the correct form is [N] to indicate 'n' is capitalized as correction in the quotation.
note: Cannot verify against actual PDF source - file '2019ND283.pdf' contains a different opinion (Jesser v. NDOT). This is flagged as medium confidence based on legal writing conventions alone.

his well-

evidence: PDF pdftotext shows hyphenated word 'well-' at end of line, with 'being' on the next line. The ingestion process converted this line break to a space, resulting in 'well- being'. The correct form is 'well-being' (hyphenated word rejoined without internal space).

old_exact: Johnson's in favor of awarding primary residential drinking weighed heavily responsibility to Lessard
new_exact: Johnson's drinking weighed heavily in favor of awarding primary residential responsibility to Lessard
source_quote: [¶15] The district court considered the best interest factors and made findings about relevant factors. It is clear from the court's findings that Johnson's drinking weighed heavily in favor of awarding primary residential responsibility to Lessard. The court found Johnson's alcoholism impaired his emotional ties with the children and his ability to tend to the children's developmental needs, and it adversely affected his relationship with the children.
evidence: The DB contains a scrambled phrase where 'in favor of awarding primary residential' and 'drinking weighed heavily' are transposed. The correct ordering 'drinking weighed heavily in favor of awarding primary residential' appears clearly in the PDF source.
note: This is a word-order error (split_join) where multiple phrases appear out of sequence, creating a grammatically incoherent sentence. The PDF clearly shows the correct word order.

old_exact: granted only for good cause shown[.]" financial
new_exact: granted only for good cause shown[.]"
source_quote: Under N.D.R.Ct. 6.1(b) a motion for a continuance "shall be promptly filed as soon as the grounds therefor are known and will be granted only for good cause shown[.]"
evidence: The DB contains a stray word 'financial' that appears after the closing quotation mark and bracket of ¶8. This word does not appear in the PDF source, which shows ¶8 ending cleanly with the bracket-enclosed period. The next paragraph [¶9] begins immediately after.
note: Stray text 'financial' appears as OCR debris or ingestion artifact at line break between ¶8 and ¶9. The PDF confirms this word is not in the original text.

old_exact: which requires the requisite disorder and "proof of a nexus between dangerousness [to] encompass[ ] proof that the disorder involves
new_exact: which requires "proof of a nexus between the requisite disorder and dangerousness [to] encompass[ ] proof that the disorder involves
source_quote: which requires "proof of a nexus between the requisite disorder and dangerousness [to] encompass[ ] proof that the disorder involves serious difficulty in controlling behavior and suffices to distinguish a dangerous sexual offender whose disorder subjects him to civil commitment from the dangerous but typical recidivist in the ordinary criminal case.
evidence: The phrase 'the requisite disorder and' appears in the wrong position in the DB text. In the DB, it reads 'which requires the requisite disorder and "proof of a nexus between dangerousness', but the official PDF clearly shows 'which requires "proof of a nexus between the requisite disorder and dangerousness'. This is a clear split/join transcription error where a phrase segment was misplaced. High-resolution PDF rendering confirms the correct text visually on page 5 of the official opinion. The corrected phrase in the source quote is identical to the new_exact text.
note: This is a substantive word-order error in the definition of a sexually dangerous individual, a critical legal standard in the opinion. It affects the meaning of the text by changing the grammatical structure of the requirement.
